questions.question: What distinguishes an AI agent from a regular chatbot?
options: Agents use a different programming language
Agents can use tools, plan multi-step tasks, and take actions autonomously
Agents are always connected to the internet
Agents only work with voice input
questions.explanation: Unlike chatbots that simply respond to messages, agents can invoke tools, break goals into steps, and act autonomously to complete complex tasks.

questions.question: Which component serves as the "brain" of an AI agent?
questions.explanation: The LLM is the reasoning core of an agent — it interprets inputs, decides what actions to take, and formulates responses.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a characteristic of AI agents?
options: They can only respond to single-turn queries
They can plan across multiple steps
They can maintain context over a task
questions.explanation: Being limited to single-turn queries is a chatbot limitation, not an agent characteristic. Agents are designed for multi-step, multi-turn tasks.
